LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY IN VIOLATION OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 835 AND GRANTED AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER THE CITY CREATED A DANGEROUS CONDITION BY PUTTING THE SIGN IN SANDY SOIL AND WHETHER THE CITY HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THE LACK OF MERIT OF HIS COMMON LAW CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN MACLEAN, TEL. NO. (510) 441-8916 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. GERCHOW, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3936 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the cause of action for dangerous condition of public property in violation of Government Code section 835 and granted as to all other claims. There are triable issues whether the City created a dangerous condition by putting the sign in sandy soil and whether the City had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his common law claims. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =302/HK
